scenario.title: The Production Guardian
scenario.context: Your app is in production and you need comprehensive monitoring, alerting, and incident response procedures. You need AI to help set up observability and create runbooks for common issues.
scenario.goal: Write a prompt that guides AI to create a production monitoring strategy with alerting rules, dashboards, and incident response procedures.
constraints: Cover application, infrastructure, and business metrics
scenario.exampleInput: Set up monitoring for a SaaS app: Next.js frontend, Node.js API, PostgreSQL. Key SLOs: 99.9% uptime, p95 latency < 200ms, error rate < 0.1%. Need alerts for on-call, runbooks for common issues.
hints: Define what metrics matter for your app
Set clear thresholds for each alert level
Think about who gets alerted and when

scoringGuide.excellent: Application, infra, and business metrics with SLOs

evaluatorContext.idealResponse:
Create a production monitoring strategy:

PRODUCTION MONITORING: SaaS Application

---

SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE:

```
                    [CloudFlare CDN]
                          |
                    [Load Balancer]
                     /          \
            [Next.js]            [Node.js API]
            (Vercel)              (Railway)
                                     |
                              [PostgreSQL]
                              (Supabase)
```

---

SERVICE LEVEL OBJECTIVES (SLOs):

```yaml
availability:
  target: 99.9%
  measurement: Successful requests / Total requests
  window: 30-day rolling
  error_budget: 43.2 minutes/month

latency:
  target: p95 < 200ms
  measurement: Request duration at 95th percentile
  window: 1-hour rolling
  
error_rate:
  target: < 0.1%
  measurement: 5xx responses / Total responses
  window: 1-hour rolling

apdex:
  target: > 0.95
  satisfied: < 100ms
  tolerating: < 500ms
```

---

METRICS TO COLLECT:

```yaml
application_metrics:
  frontend:
    - Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID, CLS)
    - Page load time by route
    - JavaScript errors
    - Client-side API call latency
    - User session duration

  api:
    - Request rate (RPM)
    - Response time (p50, p95, p99)
    - Error rate by endpoint
    - Active connections
    - Request queue depth

  database:
    - Query execution time
    - Connection pool usage
    - Slow query count (>100ms)
    - Transaction rate
    - Deadlock count

infrastructure_metrics:
  compute:
    - CPU utilization
    - Memory usage
    - Disk I/O
    - Network throughput

  containers:
    - Container restarts
    - OOM kills
    - Resource limits hit

business_metrics:
  - Active users (DAU, WAU, MAU)
  - Signup conversion rate
  - Payment success rate
  - Feature usage by plan
  - Churn indicators
```

---

MONITORING STACK:

```yaml
application_monitoring:
  tool: Sentry
  captures:
    - Errors with stack traces
    - Performance transactions
    - User feedback
    - Release tracking

infrastructure_monitoring:
  tool: Datadog / Grafana Cloud
  captures:
    - Server metrics
    - Container metrics
    - Custom dashboards
    - Log aggregation

uptime_monitoring:
  tool: Better Uptime / Checkly
  captures:
    - Endpoint availability
    - SSL certificate expiry
    - Response time trends
    - Multi-region checks

real_user_monitoring:
  tool: Vercel Analytics / PostHog
  captures:
    - Real user performance
    - User journeys
    - Feature flags impact
```

---

ALERTING RULES:

```yaml
critical_alerts:
  immediate_page:
    - name: "Site Down"
      condition: uptime_check_failed for 2 minutes
      action: Page on-call, auto-create incident
      
    - name: "Error Rate Spike"
      condition: error_rate > 5% for 5 minutes
      action: Page on-call
      
    - name: "Database Unreachable"
      condition: db_connection_failures > 0 for 1 minute
      action: Page on-call + DBA

    - name: "Payment Processing Down"
      condition: payment_success_rate < 50% for 5 minutes
      action: Page on-call + finance alert

warning_alerts:
  slack_notification:
    - name: "High Latency"
      condition: p95_latency > 500ms for 10 minutes
      action: Slack #alerts, investigate
      
    - name: "Error Rate Elevated"
      condition: error_rate > 1% for 15 minutes
      action: Slack #alerts
      
    - name: "Memory High"
      condition: memory_usage > 85% for 10 minutes
      action: Slack #alerts, may need scaling
      
    - name: "Slow Queries"
      condition: slow_query_count > 50/minute
      action: Slack #alerts, review query

info_alerts:
  daily_digest:
    - Error budget consumption rate
    - SLO compliance percentage
    - Resource utilization trends
    - Upcoming certificate expirations
```

---

ESCALATION MATRIX:

```yaml
level_1:
  who: On-call engineer
  response_time: 5 minutes
  authority: Restart services, rollback, scale
  
level_2:
  who: Senior engineer + team lead
  response_time: 15 minutes
  triggered_by: Issue not resolved in 30 minutes
  authority: All L1 + database changes, vendor contact
  
level_3:
  who: CTO + engineering leadership
  response_time: 30 minutes
  triggered_by: Customer impact > 1 hour OR data loss
  authority: All decisions, public communication

on_call_rotation:
  schedule: Weekly rotation
  handoff: Monday 9am
  coverage: 24/7
  backup: Always have secondary on-call
```

---

INCIDENT RUNBOOKS:

```yaml
runbook_1_site_down:
  trigger: "Site Down" alert
  steps:
    1. Verify alert is real:
       - Check from multiple locations (Down for Everyone?)
       - Check Vercel/Railway status pages
       - Check CloudFlare status
       
    2. Identify scope:
       - Full outage or partial?
       - Which services affected?
       - Check recent deployments
       
    3. Quick fixes (try in order):
       - Rollback last deployment (Vercel: 1 click)
       - Restart API containers (Railway dashboard)
       - Check DNS resolution
       
    4. If database related:
       - Check Supabase status
       - Verify connection strings
       - Check connection pool exhaustion
       
    5. Communicate:
       - Update status page
       - Notify affected customers if > 10 minutes
       
    6. Document:
       - Timeline of events
       - Actions taken
       - Root cause (if known)

runbook_2_high_error_rate:
  trigger: "Error Rate Spike" alert
  steps:
    1. Check Sentry for error patterns:
       - New error types?
       - Specific endpoint?
       - Specific user segment?
       
    2. Check recent changes:
       - New deployment in last hour?
       - Config changes?
       - Third-party service issues?
       
    3. If deployment related:
       - Rollback immediately
       - Verify error rate drops
       
    4. If third-party related:
       - Enable fallback/graceful degradation
       - Contact vendor if needed
       
    5. If unknown:
       - Increase logging temporarily
       - Sample failing requests for analysis

runbook_3_database_slow:
  trigger: "Slow Queries" or "High Latency" alert
  steps:
    1. Check Supabase dashboard:
       - Active queries
       - Connection count
       - CPU/memory usage
       
    2. Identify problematic queries:
       - Check pg_stat_statements
       - Look for missing indexes
       - Check for table locks
       
    3. Quick mitigations:
       - Kill long-running queries
       - Add missing index (if safe)
       - Increase connection pool size
       
    4. If connection exhaustion:
       - Check for connection leaks in code
       - Restart app to release connections
       - Review pool configuration
```

---

DASHBOARD LAYOUT:

```yaml
overview_dashboard:
  row_1_slos:
    - Availability (current vs target)
    - Latency p95 (current vs target)
    - Error rate (current vs target)
    - Error budget remaining

  row_2_traffic:
    - Requests per minute (time series)
    - Active users (real-time)
    - Geographic distribution

  row_3_performance:
    - Response time heatmap
    - Slowest endpoints
    - Database query times

  row_4_infrastructure:
    - CPU usage by service
    - Memory usage by service
    - Container health

  row_5_business:
    - Signups today
    - Payment success rate
    - Feature usage breakdown
```

---

POST-INCIDENT REVIEW:

```yaml
template:
  incident_summary:
    - Date/time and duration
    - Severity and impact
    - Services affected
    - Customer impact (users, revenue)

  timeline:
    - When detected
    - When acknowledged
    - Key actions taken
    - When resolved
    - When confirmed stable

  root_cause:
    - Technical cause
    - Contributing factors
    - Why it wasn't caught earlier

  action_items:
    - Immediate fixes (already done)
    - Short-term improvements (1-2 weeks)
    - Long-term prevention (roadmap)
    - Each item has owner and due date

  lessons_learned:
    - What went well
    - What could improve
    - Changes to runbooks needed

review_meeting:
  when: Within 48 hours of resolution
  attendees: All responders + team lead
  duration: 30-60 minutes
  output: Written post-mortem shared with team
  follow_up: Action items tracked in project board
```
